FIREHAWK ANALYTICS

Organic SEO Audit

firehawkanalytics.com.au
Prepared by Visual2Action  |  June 2026
Use ← → arrow keys or the buttons to navigate  ·  Click any screenshot to zoom
Overview

Strong product. Deep content. One visibility chain holding it back.

The whole audit in three sentences, and the three areas it covers.

The Strength

A fast, modern site (96/100 desktop speed) with more KPI, case study, and industry content than any competitor in the Australian BI space.

The Gap

Google finds Firehawk's most important pages but does not index them. And almost all authority sits on the homepage, not on the pages that sell.

The Result

Buyers searching right now for what Firehawk sells cannot find it. Every finding in this audit traces one step of that problem, with data and a fix.

1

Technical & Crawling

Why pages are discovered but not indexed: content rendering, internal linking, and crawl signals.

2

Schema & Content

How search engines and AI systems understand the site: structured data and extractability.

3

Authority & Opportunity

Backlink profile gaps and the high-intent keyword demand Firehawk is not yet capturing.

Keyword Research Snapshot

The search demand this audit is built around

Primary and secondary keyword targets mapped for the Homepage and the Allied Health page, based on Australian search volume (SV) and keyword difficulty (KD). These targets shape the priorities in every section that follows.

Homepage Targets
KeywordSVKDRoleRecommendation
business intelligence consulting1406PrimaryThe website focuses on helping businesses plan, build, and implement BI solutions. It also has low competition (KD 6), making it easier to rank.
business intelligence software32053SecondaryHas the highest search volume and targets businesses looking for business intelligence solutions. It is a valuable keyword for increasing visibility.
data integration platform1703SecondaryAligns with the company's data integration services and can help to attract businesses looking to connect and manage data from multiple sources.
business intelligence dashboard11033SecondaryIt closely matches one of the company's core offerings and attracts users looking for dashboard solutions and data visualization.
business intelligence reporting software11051SecondaryTargets users looking for reporting and analytics solutions, making it highly relevant to the company's reporting capabilities.
bi & analytics software20–SupportingIt covers another common way people search for business intelligence software.
Allied Health Page Targets
KeywordSV (AU)KDRoleRecommendation
allied health analytics10 (low)–PrimaryIt describes exactly what Firehawk sells to this vertical. Volume is low today, but there is no real competition, so we can own this term early and grow with it as the category matures.
allied health practice management software17018SecondaryThe highest volume term in this space and the software our buyers already use every day. Firehawk is not a PMS, but covering PMS reporting and integrations (Cliniko, Halaxy, Nookal) puts us in front of the exact audience, and KD 18 makes it very reachable.
allied health software11026SecondaryBroad category term with solid volume. It captures clinic owners early in their research, and KD 26 is manageable for a focused vertical page like ours.
allied health reporting softwareLow / not tracked–SecondaryAlmost no tracked volume, but this is the highest intent term in the set: someone searching this has the exact problem Firehawk solves. GSC impressions confirm real demand that keyword tools do not track.
allied health software australia5013SupportingLocal variant with the easiest KD in the set (13). Australian buyers qualify themselves with this search, which fits Firehawk perfectly. Worth covering on the same page, not a separate one.
allied health practice management software australia11022SupportingSame intent as the main PMS keyword with a local qualifier. Ranking for the parent term usually wins this one too, so it needs a mention in the content, not its own page.
Where Firehawk Already Wins

SEO and technical strengths worth protecting

Before the findings, the foundation. These are real, measured advantages. The job of this audit is to make sure Google and AI systems can actually see them.

96/100

Desktop Performance Score

PageSpeed Insights: SEO 100, Best Practices 100, Accessibility 91. A technical baseline most SaaS sites never reach.

CLICK TO ZOOM
PageSpeed Insights, Desktop: Performance 96, Accessibility 91, Best Practices 100, SEO 100
✓

Indexable architecture

Every URL in the crawl sample returns a clean indexable status. No robots blocks, no noindex traps, no canonical conflicts standing in the way.

✓

Content depth competitors lack

100+ KPI definition pages, quantified case studies, and an active founder-led blog. This is a content asset base most rivals would need years to replicate.

✓

Schema foundation in place

Organization, SoftwareApplication, WebSite, and FAQPage schema already exist on the site. They need connecting, not creating.

✓

Clean, penalty-free link profile

A brand-first backlink profile means zero toxic-link risk. It is a safe, trusted base to build commercial authority on.

✓

Clear product architecture

Insights Engine and Infrastructure Studio, plus a focused vertical play in allied health. Clear structure makes topical authority achievable.

✓

Modern, fixable stack

The site runs on Next.js. Every issue raised in this audit is an implementation change, not a rebuild.

Technical Fixes - Crawling Issues

Finding 1: Discovered But Not Indexed (35 Urls)

Problem

Here, we are mentioning few examples:

Example No 1: Under Resources section, /academy page, weak content. If it is going to be hub in the future then this page should need other sections as well. Google already knows the URL exists. Now it needs stronger signals that the page is important. Right now google is not seeing worth enough to allocate budget to crawl them. Check the image below. GSC is proving that it can find these pages.

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/academy  (Thin Content, Low Authority Page) Just Title, FAQ, and CTA

Technical Fixes - Crawling Issues

Findings: Discovered But Not Indexed

Problem

Example No 2: Some of the Blogs are found either irrelevant by the solution we are covering, also it has no internal linking and no external linkings. For example this article: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/blog/poke-the-box. Seth Godin (A marketing guru)

This blog doesn't link to:

  • Industry pages
  • Solution pages
  • Related blogs

Seth Godin's Blog: https://seths.blog/

Why it matters

Without contextual internal links, blog posts become isolated content that does not help distribute authority across the website. It is called “orphan” (No parent, No sibling references). Also, we need to publish blogs that provide genuine value to the audience in a very personalized way so even though our domain might be new, google will trust our page and help to allocate crawl budget.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/blog/poke-the-box

CLICK TO ZOOM
/blog/poke-the-box: no contextual internal links
CLICK TO ZOOM
Google Search Console status
Solution

We should Focus blog topics on customer problems that Firehawk Analytics solves mainly in different ways. Also within article, we can naturally add contextual links to related Solution, Industry, KPI, and Platform pages where they add value to the reader. If google constantly sees this pattern that not discovered page will be indexed since google will not treat as orphan any longer.

Technical Fixes - Crawling Issues

Finding 2: Important pages rely on client-side rendering

Problem

Pages containing permalinks https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/industries  https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/solutions pages (27 pages in total) load the main content with JavaScript instead of including it in the initial HTML. This can make it harder for Google to crawl and index the pages efficiently. That is why Google does not spend enough time crawling these pages, so they remain "Discovered - currently not indexed" in Google Search Console. This is a common issue with many Next.js websites.

Google Support: Next.js site not being indexed Total Affected URLs: Discovered But Not Crawled

Why it matters

Google can read JavaScript, but it needs an extra step before it can fully understand the page. On a newer website with lower authority, this can delay the crawling and indexing of important commercial pages.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/industries

CLICK TO ZOOM
/industries: main content loaded via JavaScript
CLICK TO ZOOM
Google Search Console: affected URLs
Related: Mobile Speed

Client-side rendering is also holding back the mobile experience. PageSpeed Insights currently scores mobile performance at 60/100 (desktop is 96/100). Fixing this rendering issue will also help the mobile speed score.

CLICK TO ZOOM
PageSpeed Insights, Mobile: Performance 60. SEO 100 and Best Practices 100 remain strong.
Solution

Work with the developer to make sure these pages load their main content immediately instead of showing a loading screen first. This allows Google to read the page more easily.

Is it fixable?
Yes. If the site is built with Next.js (which your HTML indicates), this is a common implementation change. Depending on how those pages are built, the developer may be able to:

  • Server-side render (SSR) the page.
  • Use static generation (SSG) where appropriate.
  • Ensure important content and links are included in the initial HTML.
Technical Fixes - Crawling Issues

Finding 3: Key hub pages receive limited site-wide internal links

Problem

Pages containing permalinks https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/industries  https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/solutions and it's child pages are not included in the site's footer navigation. Instead, they are primarily accessed through the main navigation, resulting in fewer consistent internal links across the site

Why it matters

Internal links help Google understand which pages are most important. Pages that receive links from many parts of the website are generally easier for Google to crawl and evaluate.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/solutions

Total Affected URLs: Discovered But Not Crawled

Evidence Screenshots
CLICK TO ZOOM
/solutions page
CLICK TO ZOOM
Footer navigation: hub pages missing
Solution

These pages need internal links from more places across the website. Two steps: add /industries and /solutions to the footer navigation, and link to them naturally from blogs, KPI pages, case studies, and other related content. The more high-quality internal links these pages receive, the stronger the signal to Google that they are important pages worth crawling and indexing.

Technical Fixes - Schema Issues

Finding 4: Homepage Schema Has Invalid customer property

Problem

The property customer is not recognised for LocalBusiness.

Why it matters

Because this property is not valid, search engines may ignore the list of customers. As a result, these client references may not be understood or used as intended.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/ (Homepage)

CLICK TO ZOOM
Schema validation: invalid customer property flagged
CLICK TO ZOOM
Homepage LocalBusiness schema detail
Solution

Remove the unsupported customer property from the homepage schema, as it is not recognised by Schema.org.

If the goal is to showcase client relationships, create dedicated Case Study pages instead. These pages can use valid structured data (such as Article or CreativeWork) and reference the client organisation using supported Schema.org properties like about or mentions.

This helps search engines understand the relationship correctly while also creating valuable content that can rank independently.

Technical Fixes - Schema Issues

Finding 5: Schema entities are not connected

Problem

The homepage contains multiple schema types, including Organization, SoftwareApplication, WebSite, and FAQPage, but they are not connected using shared entity references (@id)

Why it matters

Search engines and AI systems understand websites more effectively when related schema objects are connected. Without these relationships, each schema type is interpreted separately instead of as part of one business entity. This makes it harder for search engines to understand how the company, its products, and its content relate to each other.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/ (Homepage)

CLICK TO ZOOM
Homepage schema types detected
CLICK TO ZOOM
No shared @id references between entities
Solution

We need to assign a unique @id to the Organization schema and reference that same @id from related schema types, such as SoftwareApplication, WebSite, FAQPage, and other relevant schemas across the website. This creates a connected entity graph that helps search engines and AI systems better understand the business and its products.

Technical Fixes - Schema Issues

Finding 6: Service page missing FAQ Schema

Problem

The Allied Health service page contains FAQ content but does not include FAQPage schema.

Why it matters

Google no longer shows FAQ rich results for most websites but FAQ schema is still valuable. It helps search engines and AI assistants understand the page's content, supports entity recognition, and makes it easier for AI-powered search experiences to identify and reference relevant questions and answers.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/industries/allied-health (Allied Health Service Page)

CLICK TO ZOOM
Allied Health page: visible FAQ content without FAQPage schema
Solution

We need to Add FAQPage schema to the existing FAQ section on the page, ensuring the structured data matches the visible content. This helps search engines and AI assistants better understand the page while keeping the content eligible for future search features.

Technical Fixes | Others

Additional Observation: areaServed limited to Australia

Problem

The Organization schema specifies Australia as the only areaServed.

Why it matters

If Firehawk Analytics also serves customers in other countries, the schema currently does not reflect that, which may limit how search engines understand the company's service coverage.

Evidence

URL: https://firehawkanalytics.com.au/ (Homepage)

CLICK TO ZOOM
Organization schema: areaServed set to Australia only
Solution

We need to update the areaServed property to accurately reflect all regions or countries where the business provides services.

Technical Fixes | Others

Additional Observation: One FAQ answer contains an incomplete price range

Problem

One FAQ answer contains an incomplete price range
"typically range from ,000 to ,000"

Evidence
CLICK TO ZOOM
FAQ answer with missing currency values
Solution

Correct the pricing values so the FAQ displays the intended price range.

Content Issues

Finding: Key pages are not fully optimized for AI extractability

Problem

The website provides valuable information, but much of it is fragmented or not presented in a way that is easy for search engines and AI systems to understand and reference. Important product details, pricing, integrations, benefits, and FAQs are often spread across different sections or rely on interactive elements instead of being clearly available as structured, self-contained content.

Why it matters

Search engines and AI assistants are increasingly looking for clear, well-structured information they can easily understand and cite. Pages that present complete, self-contained information have a better chance of being referenced in AI-generated answers and helping search engines better understand the business, its products, and its expertise.

Solution

We have created Allied Health page as a template for improving this and same replicating to other important pages across the website, including the homepage, solution pages, platform pages, and industry pages. Open the link and you see that we have focused on making important information easy to understand by both users and machines through:

  • Clear, self-contained explanations of products and services.
  • Important facts, pricing, integrations, and benefits presented in plain text rather than relying on interactive elements.
  • Complete FAQ content available in the initial HTML

Applying this approach consistently across the website will improve content clarity, strengthen entity understanding, and increase the likelihood of search engines and AI assistants referencing the website's content.

Open the Allied Health template demo

Backlink Analysis

Finding 1: Backlink profile is mostly brand-focused

Problem

Most backlinks use branded anchors such as "Firehawk Analytics", "Firehawk", or the website URL. There are very few backlinks using keywords related to the services or solutions the business wants to rank for.

Evidence
CLICK TO ZOOM
Anchor text distribution: branded anchors dominate
CLICK TO ZOOM
Anchor breakdown detail
Solution

We must build high-quality backlinks using natural, keyword-relevant anchor text through guest posts, digital PR, case studies, and industry resources. And, we should focus on commercial pages instead of linking only to the homepage.

Backlink Analysis

Finding 2: Commercial pages have almost no backlinks

Problem

Most backlinks point to the homepage, while important commercial pages such as Industry, Solution, and Platform pages have little or no backlinks

Why it matters

Commercial pages need external authority to compete for industry and service-related keywords. Without backlinks, Google has fewer signals that these pages are important and trustworthy. This is important for ranking as well!

Our links: Firehawk Analytics Backlinks

Evidence
CLICK TO ZOOM
Backlink distribution: homepage concentration, commercial pages near zero
Solution

Just like previous section, we discussed that we must build backlinks directly to important commercial pages using:

  • Industry guest posts
  • Healthcare and business directories
  • Digital PR
  • Partner websites
  • Relevant resource pages
  • Case study mentions
Backlink Analysis

Finding 3: Competitors have stronger industry-specific backlinks

Problem

Competitors have earned backlinks from highly relevant industry websites, while Firehawk has very limited presence on these authoritative sources.

Evidence

See the links that competitors have, where we can build our links/presence.

Competitor Gap

Solution

We can develop an outreach campaign focused on earning links from:

  • Healthcare associations
  • Industry publications
  • Accounting and finance communities
  • Business intelligence publications

We can focus on earning links that are relevant to the industries Firehawk serves.

Opportunity: Capture High-Intent Customers

Buyers are already searching. They just cannot find Firehawk yet.

The Opportunity

Many potential customers are already searching for reporting solutions for the software they use every day. However, they are unlikely to search for Firehawk Analytics because they may not know the brand exists yet.

This creates an opportunity to build dedicated landing pages that answer these searches and introduce Firehawk at the exact moment businesses are looking for a better reporting solution. A sample page structure has been prepared to demonstrate this approach.

Why it matters

Instead of competing only for broad keywords like Business Intelligence or waiting for branded searches, Firehawk can attract highly qualified visitors searching for topics such as:

Cliniko Reporting Halaxy Reporting Nookal Reporting Firehawk vs Dataplayer Dataplayer Alternative

These visitors are already trying to solve a reporting or analytics problem, making them much more likely to become customers.

This approach also helps build topical authority and creates content that is easier for search engines and AI assistants to understand and reference.

Opportunity: Capture High-Intent Customers

The play: dedicated comparison and reporting pages

Solution

Create a series of dedicated comparison and reporting pages using a consistent page structure similar to the demonstration.

Each page should include:

  • A clear explanation of the software and its reporting capabilities.
  • A balanced comparison showing where Firehawk adds value.
  • Feature comparison tables.
  • Real dashboard examples and screenshots.
  • Common use cases.
  • Frequently asked questions.
  • Links to relevant Industry, Platform, KPI, and Case Study pages.

Once the structure has been validated, the same approach can be expanded across other software platforms, industries, and competitor comparison pages to build a scalable content strategy.

There is a group of potential customers already searching for the problems you solve. Right now, they can't find you. Let's create the content that helps them discover Firehawk.

Quick Fixes | Screaming Frog Crawl

Three symptoms. One root cause. One developer fix.

The full-site crawl surfaced only one issue class that genuinely matters, and it matters a lot. On 100+ URLs across the blog, case study, and KPI sections, the three most important SEO tags are rendering outside the <head> of the page. When the <head> closes early, Google can treat these tags as ordinary body content and ignore them.

1

Page titles render outside the <head>

The <title> tag appears in the <body> on 100+ crawled URLs. Google may disregard it and rewrite titles on its own, weakening relevance signals for every affected page.

100+ URLsHigh ImpactLow Effort
Full URL list: Page Titles Outside Head
2

Meta descriptions render outside the <head>

The same URLs carry well-written meta descriptions that Google may never read, because they sit outside the <head>. That effort is currently invisible to search engines.

100+ URLsHigh ImpactLow Effort
Full URL list: Meta Descriptions Outside Head
3

Canonical tags render outside the <head>

Canonical tags are not being picked up inside the <head> on the same URL set. A canonical that Google cannot read leaves duplicate-handling and URL consolidation entirely to Google's guesswork.

100+ URLsHigh ImpactLow Effort
Full URL list: Canonicals Outside Head
The Fix

This is one implementation change, not three. In Next.js, an invalid element inside the <head> (or metadata injected after hydration) causes browsers and crawlers to close the <head> early, pushing everything after it into the <body>. The developer should emit title, meta description, and canonical through the Next.js Metadata API so they are present inside the <head> of the initial HTML, then verify with view-source. It is the same root cause as the rendering issue in Finding 2, which means one sprint fixes both.

Connecting The Dots

How every finding in this audit helps Firehawk rank

Every fix we covered feeds the same loop. We fix them in this order, and each one makes the next one stronger. Once the loop is running, it compounds on its own.

One fix alone helps a little. All of them together start the loop. The more we rank, the more links we earn, and the faster Google crawls us. That is how Firehawk starts to compound.
